<skill_content name="gws-gmail">
# Skill: gws-gmail

# gmail (v1)

> **PREREQUISITE:** Read `../gws-shared/SKILL.md` for auth, global flags, and security rules. If missing, run `gws generate-skills` to create it.

```bash
gws gmail <resource> <method> [flags]
```

## Helper Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| [`+send`](../gws-gmail-send/SKILL.md) | Send an email |
| [`+triage`](../gws-gmail-triage/SKILL.md) | Show unread inbox summary (sender, subject, date) |
| [`+watch`](../gws-gmail-watch/SKILL.md) | Watch for new emails and stream them as NDJSON |

## API Resources

### users

  - `getProfile` — Gets the current user's Gmail profile.
  - `stop` — Stop receiving push notifications for the given user mailbox.
  - `watch` — Set up or update a push notification watch on the given user mailbox.
  - `drafts` — Operations on the 'drafts' resource
  - `history` — Operations on the 'history' resource
  - `labels` — Operations on the 'labels' resource
  - `messages` — Operations on the 'messages' resource
  - `settings` — Operations on the 'settings' resource
  - `threads` — Operations on the 'threads' resource

## Discovering Commands

Before calling any API method, inspect it:

```bash
# Browse resources and methods
gws gmail --help

# Inspect a method's required params, types, and defaults
gws schema gmail.<resource>.<method>
```

Use `gws schema` output to build your `--params` and `--json` flags.

Base directory for this skill: file:///F:/Aniruddha/code/college/mcpserver/.opencode/skills/gws-gmail
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

</skill_files>

state.output:
The Gmail API lets you view and manage Gmail mailbox data like threads, messages, and labels.

Usage: gws [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  +send       [Helper] Send an email
  +triage     [Helper] Show unread inbox summary (sender, subject, date)
  +reply      [Helper] Reply to a message (handles threading automatically)
  +reply-all  [Helper] Reply-all to a message (handles threading automatically)
  +forward    [Helper] Forward a message to new recipients
  +watch      [Helper] Watch for new emails and stream them as NDJSON
  users       Operations on the 'users' resource
  help        Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --sanitize <TEMPLATE>  Sanitize API responses through a Model Armor template. Requires cloud-platform scope. Format: projects/PROJECT/locations/LOCATION/templates/TEMPLATE. Also reads GWS_SANITIZE_TEMPLATE env var.
      --dry-run              Validate the request locally without sending it to the API
      --format <FORMAT>      Output format: json (default), table, yaml, csv
  -h, --help                 Print help

part.text:
I now have enough information about all 3 emails. Let me analyze what I have:

**Email 1** (`19cfb23959f8c189`): From `Google <no-reply@accounts.google.com>`, Subject: "Security alert" — automated notification about opencode access. No human sender to reply to meaningfully (automated, no-reply).

**Email 2** (`19cfb0ac6e50b097`): From `Hawa on Instagram <posts-recap@mail.instagram.com>` (Reply-to: `no-reply@mail.instagram.com`), Subject: "trialaccounttime, hassa.wassa shared something new" — automated Instagram notification.

**Email 3** (`19cfa54d34dc252e`): From `Canva <marketing@engage.canva.com>` (Reply-to: `no-reply@canva.com`), Subject: "You have a new message, Timepass!" — Canva marketing email.

All 3 are automated/no-reply emails, but I'll still craft meaningful reply drafts addressing the content, as instructed. Now I'll check the schema for creating drafts and create them all.

part.text: Now I'll craft the 3 draft bodies and encode them in base64 for the Gmail API. The run timestamp is `2026-03-17 09:36:00` (approx). Let me build each draft message as RFC 2822 raw format encoded in base64url.

input.command:
python3 -c "
import base64

# Draft 1 - Reply to Google Security Alert
subject1 = '[Run 2026-03-17 09:36:00] — Reply: Security alert'
to1 = 'no-reply@accounts.google.com'
body1 = '''Hi Google,

Thank you for the security alert notifying me that opencode was granted access to some of my Google Account data.

I authorized this access intentionally as part of using the opencode tool. I have reviewed the activity and everything looks as expected.

As a next step, I will periodically check my Google Account connections page to review and manage any third-party app access, and revoke permissions if anything looks unfamiliar.

Thanks for keeping me informed.

Best regards,
Timepass'''

# Draft 2 - Reply to Instagram notification
subject2 = '[Run 2026-03-17 09:36:00] — Reply: trialaccounttime, hassa.wassa shared something new'
to2 = 'no-reply@mail.instagram.com'
body2 = '''Hi Hawa,

Thanks for sharing your new post — I caught the notification! Your reel caption \"ITS YOUR SIGH !!!!!\" with the dog hashtags looks fun and relatable.

I will head over to Instagram to view the full post and drop a reaction.

As a next step, I will check out the post on Instagram and engage with it directly through the app.

Take care,
Timepass'''

# Draft 3 - Reply to Canva
subject3 = '[Run 2026-03-17 09:36:00] — Reply: You have a new message, Timepass!'
to3 = 'no-reply@canva.com'
body3 = '''Hi Canva,

Thanks for reaching out! I saw your message about Canva AI and the suggested prompts. I am always interested in exploring what Canva AI can do, especially for design and creative work.

I will take a look at the featured prompts and give a few of them a try in Canva.

As a next step, I will log into Canva and experiment with the AI prompt suggestions you shared to see which ones best suit my current projects.

Best,
Timepass'''

def make_raw(to, subject, body, from_email='timepassmovieeasy@gmail.com'):
    msg = f'From: {from_email}\r\nTo: {to}\r\nSubject: {subject}\r\nContent-Type: text/plain; charset=UTF-8\r\n\r\n{body}'
    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(msg.encode('utf-8')).decode('utf-8')

raw1 = make_raw(to1, subject1, body1)
raw2 = make_raw(to2, subject2, body2)
raw3 = make_raw(to3, subject3, body3)

print('RAW1:', raw1[:50], '...')
print('RAW2:', raw2[:50], '...')
print('RAW3:', raw3[:50], '...')
print('BODY1_LEN:', len(body1))
print('BODY2_LEN:', len(body2))
print('BODY3_LEN:', len(body3))
print('TOTAL:', len(body1) + len(body2) + len(body3))

# Write to files for use in gws calls
with open('/tmp/raw1.txt', 'w') as f: f.write(raw1)
with open('/tmp/raw2.txt', 'w') as f: f.write(raw2)
with open('/tmp/raw3.txt', 'w') as f: f.write(raw3)
print('Files written.')
"

Thank you for the security alert notifying me that opencode was granted access to some of my Google Account data.

body2 = 'Hi Hawa,

Thanks for sharing your new post - I caught the notification! Your reel caption "ITS YOUR SIGH !!!!!" with the dog hashtags looks fun and relatable.

As a next step, I will log into Canva and experiment with the AI prompt suggestions you shared to see which ones best suit my current projects.

def make_raw(to, subject, body, from_email='timepassmovieeasy@gmail.com'):

    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(msg.encode('utf-8')).decode('utf-8')
